text:
Wei-Min Wang
Wei-Min Wang is a Visiting Professor of Mathematics at NYU Shanghai. Wei-Min is a research director in the Centre National de la Recherche Scientifique (CNRS) in France. She received her B.S. from MIT and PhD from Princeton University. Wei-Min has been a member at the Institute for Advanced Study, Princeton. She was a plenary speaker at the International Congress of Mathematical Physics. Wei-Min's research interests mainly lie in analysis, partial differential equations, infinite dimensional dynamical systems and mathematical physics.
